title: Running multiple rootless containers in parallel with keep-ns sometimes fails with "runc: runc create failed: User namespaces enabled, but no user mapping found"
body:
### Issue Description

Running multiple rootless podman containers in parallel with keep-id on sometimes results in some of the containers failing with the following error:

> {"cause":"OCI runtime error","message":"runc: runc create failed: User namespaces enabled, but no user mapping found.: OCI runtime error","response":500}

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Setup rootless podman
2. Run the following script

```bash
#!/bin/bash -e

DOCKER_HOST="/run/user/$(id -u)/podman/podman.sock"

run() {
    IMAGE_NAME=$1
    CONTAINER_NAME=$2
    CONTAINER_CONFIG="{\"Image\":\"$IMAGE_NAME\",\"Cmd\": [],\"AttachStdin\": false,\"AttachStdout\": true,\"AttachStderr\": true,\"Tty\": false}"
    
    curl -s -H "Content-Type: application/json" --unix-socket "$DOCKER_HOST" "http://localhost/containers/create?name=$CONTAINER_NAME" --data "$CONTAINER_CONFIG"

    curl -s -X POST --unix-socket "$DOCKER_HOST" "http://localhost/containers/$CONTAINER_NAME/start"

    podman inspect "$CONTAINER_NAME"
}

echo "Pulling all images"

podman pull ubuntu:20.04 &
podman pull ubuntu:22.04 &
podman pull ubuntu:23.04 &
podman pull ubuntu:23.10

echo "Running all images"

podman rm -f test0 test1 test2 test3

run ubuntu:20.04 test0 &
run ubuntu:22.04 test1 &
run ubuntu:23.04 test2 &
run ubuntu:23.10 test3

podman stop test0 test1 test2 test3
podman rm -f test0 test1 test2 test3

```

### Describe the results you received

Some containers randomly fail with the following error:

> {"cause":"OCI runtime error","message":"runc: runc create failed: User namespaces enabled, but no user mapping found.: OCI runtime error","response":500}

### Describe the results you expected

Containers can run in parallel

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

> $ cat /etc/redhat-release
> Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.8 (Ootpa)

> $ podman version
> Client:       Podman Engine
> Version:      4.4.1
> API Version:  4.4.1
> Go Version:   go1.19.6
> Built:        Thu Jun 15 10:39:56 2023
> OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

> $ cat /etc/subuid
> myuser:200000:100000

> $ cat /etc/subgid
> myuser:200000:65536

> $ cat /usr/share/containers/containers.conf | grep userns
> userns = "keep-id"
> #userns_size = 65536

### Additional information

The user podman socket is perpetually running setup using the following commands:

```
/usr/bin/loginctl enable-linger myuser
/usr/bin/systemctl --user enable --now podman.socket
/usr/bin/systemctl --user start podman.service
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Have you been able to nail this down with a repeater?

For the most part. The script above almost always yields the runc error for me for at least one of the executions but usually 3 out of 4 of them. I'm not versed in what is going on behind the scenes but I suspect the combination of keep-id + namespace remapping that I am using has problems with running in parallel and the only time I don't see 3/4 errors is when of the images terminates in time proving room for another one to start.

After further experimentation I've observed that this shows up for every container that succeeds:

```
"IDMappings": {
     "UidMap": [
          "0:1:78572",
          "78572:0:1",
          "78573:78573:21428"
     ],
     "GidMap": [
          "0:1:20",
          "20:0:1",
          "21:21:65516"
     ]
```
     
This shows up for every container that fails:
```
"IDMappings": {
     "UidMap": [
          "78572:0:1"
     ],
     "GidMap": [
          "20:0:1"
     ]
},
```

I increased the subuid/subgid size to see if that would resolve the issue. 

> $ cat /etc/subuid
> myuser:200000:1000000
> 
> $ cat /etc/subgid
> myuser:200000:655360

This only resulted in the IDMappings filling the rest of the range:

```
"IDMappings": {
     "UidMap": [
          "0:1:78572",
          "78572:0:1",
          "78573:78573:921428"
     ],
     "GidMap": [
          "0:1:20",
          "20:0:1",
          "21:21:655340"
     ]
},
```

When running in rootless and keep-id is on is the first container consuming all of the available namespaced uid/gid values resulting in parallel executions failing because of not having any values left?

It seems that containers created in parallel using the create API do not properly setup GID/UID mappings. Either I'm missing some parameter when calling the API or the CLI behaves differently.

This works in parallel. Each created container has IDMappings properly filled out:

> podman create --name "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$IMAGE_NAME"

```
"IDMappings": {
     "UidMap": [
          "0:1:78572",
          "78572:0:1",
          "78573:78573:921428"
     ],
     "GidMap": [
          "0:1:20",
          "20:0:1",
          "21:21:655340"
     ]
},
```

This does NOT work. Some of the containers are missing mappings data in the "IDMappings" field:

> curl -s -H "Content-Type: application/json" --unix-socket "$DOCKER_HOST" "http://localhost/containers/create?name=$CONTAINER_NAME" --data "$CONTAINER_CONFIG"

Containers with missing mapping config:
```
"IDMappings": {
     "UidMap": [
          "78572:0:1"
     ],
     "GidMap": [
          "20:0:1"
     ]
},
```

Working script using podman create:

```
#!/bin/bash -e

DOCKER_HOST="/run/user/$(id -u)/podman/podman.sock"

run() {
    IMAGE_NAME=$1
    CONTAINER_NAME=$2
    
    podman create --name "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$IMAGE_NAME"

    podman inspect "$CONTAINER_NAME"
}

echo "Pulling all images"

podman pull ubuntu:20.04 &
podman pull ubuntu:22.04 &
podman pull ubuntu:23.04 &
podman pull ubuntu:23.10

echo "Running all images"

podman rm -f test0 test1 test2 test3

run ubuntu:20.04 test0 &
run ubuntu:22.04 test1 &
run ubuntu:23.04 test2 &
run ubuntu:23.10 test3

podman stop test0 test1 test2 test3
podman rm -f test0 test1 test2 test3
```

I let the reproducer script run for over an hour, but I am not able to reproduce locally on RHEL with Podman 4.4.1.

Can you reproduce this issue with the latest upstream release?

If it happens only RHEL then I suggest filing a bug using the official channels
Given there was never an answer closing
